stem: A production service has high tail latency only during garbage collection pauses. What deployment-level mitigation can reduce user impact while engineers tune memory behavior?
options: Use horizontal scaling with enough replicas, readiness handling, and load balancing so one paused instance does not dominate request latency.
Pin all traffic to one large instance so garbage collection has more heap to use.
Disable health checks because pauses are temporary and should not affect routing.
Increase log verbosity during every request so garbage collection has more data to analyze.
explanation: Multiple replicas and routing only to ready instances reduce the blast radius of pauses while deeper memory tuning proceeds. Single large instances, disabled health checks, and extra logging can worsen tail latency.
